| **[Anzac Test](/wiki/Anzac_Test "Anzac Test")**  ← [2015](/wiki/2015_Anzac_Test "2015 Anzac Test")  [2017](/wiki/2017_Anzac_Test "2017 Anzac Test") → | |

The **2016 Anzac Test** (known as the **[Downer](/wiki/Downer_Group "Downer Group") Anzac Test** due to sponsorship) was a [rugby league test match](/wiki/Test_match_(rugby_league) "Test match (rugby league)") played between [Australia](/wiki/Australian_Kangaroos "Australian Kangaroos") and [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand_national_rugby_league_team "New Zealand national rugby league team") at [Hunter Stadium](/wiki/Newcastle_International_Sports_Centre "Newcastle International Sports Centre") in [Newcastle](/wiki/Newcastle,_New_South_Wales "Newcastle, New South Wales").[[1]](#cite_note-1) It was the 17th [Anzac Test](/wiki/Anzac_Test "Anzac Test") played between the two nations since the first was played under the [Super League](/wiki/Super_League_(Australia) "Super League (Australia)") banner in [1997](/wiki/1997_Anzac_Test "1997 Anzac Test").

The game marked the international coaching debut of [Mal Meninga](/wiki/Mal_Meninga "Mal Meninga") for Australia since taking over the position from [Tim Sheens](/wiki/Tim_Sheens "Tim Sheens") in late 2015. Meninga, a 4 time [Kangaroo Tourist](/wiki/Kangaroo_Tour "Kangaroo Tour") (the only player ever to do so), a [World Cup](/wiki/Rugby_League_World_Cup "Rugby League World Cup") winning captain and a veteran of 46 tests for Australia between 1982–94, has previously coached [Queensland](/wiki/Queensland_Maroons "Queensland Maroons") to 9 [State of Origin series](/wiki/State_of_Origin_series "State of Origin series") wins in 10 years (including a record 8 series wins in succession from 2006-2013) in the years prior to taking over as Kangaroos coach.

This was the first of two matches between Australia and New Zealand before the [2016 Rugby League Four Nations](/wiki/2016_Rugby_League_Four_Nations "2016 Rugby League Four Nations") in late October and November. The [second match](/wiki/2016_Rugby_League_Trans-Tasman_Test_Match "2016 Rugby League Trans-Tasman Test Match") will be played at the [nib Stadium](/wiki/Perth_Oval "Perth Oval") in [Perth](/wiki/Perth "Perth"), Western Australia on [15 October](/wiki/2016_Rugby_League_Trans-Tasman_Test_Match "2016 Rugby League Trans-Tasman Test Match").

Meninga broke tradition and named the Australian team a few days early, which included 4 debutants; [Blake Ferguson](/wiki/Blake_Ferguson_(rugby_league) "Blake Ferguson (rugby league)"), [Josh McGuire](/wiki/Josh_McGuire "Josh McGuire"), [Michael Morgan](/wiki/Michael_Morgan_(rugby_league_born_1991) "Michael Morgan (rugby league born 1991)") and Fijian-born [Semi Radradra](/wiki/Semi_Radradra "Semi Radradra").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

The New Zealand team was named on 1 May.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

1 - [Peta Hiku](/wiki/Peta_Hiku "Peta Hiku") was originally selected to play but withdrew due to injury. He was replaced by [Gerard Beale](/wiki/Gerard_Beale "Gerard Beale").  
2 - [Shaun Kenny-Dowall](/wiki/Shaun_Kenny-Dowall "Shaun Kenny-Dowall") and [Brad Takairangi](/wiki/Brad_Takairangi "Brad Takairangi") were originally selected to play but both players withdrew. [Tohu Harris](/wiki/Tohu_Harris "Tohu Harris") was shifted from five-eighth to centre and [Kodi Nikorima](/wiki/Kodi_Nikorima "Kodi Nikorima") was shifted from the bench to five-eighth as a result.

* [Aidan Guerra](/wiki/Aidan_Guerra "Aidan Guerra") and [Michael Jennings](/wiki/Michael_Jennings_(rugby_league) "Michael Jennings (rugby league)") were a part of the Kangaroos squad but did not play in the match.
* [Alex Glenn](/wiki/Alex_Glenn "Alex Glenn") and [Danny Levi](/wiki/Danny_Levi "Danny Levi") were a part of the Kiwis squad but did not play in the match.

## Match summary

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2016_Anzac_Test&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Match summary")]

|  |
| --- |
| 6 May 2016 8.05pm (AEST) |

| [Australia](/wiki/Australia_national_rugby_league_team "Australia national rugby league team") | 16–0 | [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand_national_rugby_league_team "New Zealand national rugby league team") |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Tries:** [Boyd](/wiki/Darius_Boyd "Darius Boyd") (14') **1** [Inglis](/wiki/Greg_Inglis "Greg Inglis") (32') **1** [Ferguson](/wiki/Blake_Ferguson_(rugby_league) "Blake Ferguson (rugby league)") (80') **1** **Goals:** [Thurston](/wiki/Johnathan_Thurston "Johnathan Thurston") **1/3** (20' pen) [Smith](/wiki/Cameron_Smith_(rugby_league,_born_1983) "Cameron Smith (rugby league, born 1983)") **1/2** (44' pen) **Sin Bin:** [Radradra](/wiki/Semi_Radradra "Semi Radradra") **1** (6') | [[4]](#cite_note-4) |  |

|  |
| --- |
| [Hunter Stadium](/wiki/Newcastle_International_Sports_Centre "Newcastle International Sports Centre"), [Newcastle](/wiki/Newcastle,_New_South_Wales "Newcastle, New South Wales") Attendance: 27,724 Referee: [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia") Gerard Sutton Player of the Match: [Paul Gallen](/wiki/Paul_Gallen "Paul Gallen") |

**Notes:**

* This was the annual 2016 Anzac Test.
* Australia's win ended a 3-match losing streak against New Zealand.
* This was the first time New Zealand were held scoreless in a test-match since 2007.[[5]](#cite_note-5)
* With the victory, Australia reclaim the Bill Kelly Memorial Trophy.
* [Martin Taupau](/wiki/Martin_Taupau "Martin Taupau") and [Sam Moa](/wiki/Sam_Moa "Sam Moa") made their 10th test appearance for New Zealand.
* [Blake Ferguson](/wiki/Blake_Ferguson_(rugby_league) "Blake Ferguson (rugby league)"), [Josh McGuire](/wiki/Josh_McGuire "Josh McGuire"), [Michael Morgan](/wiki/Michael_Morgan_(rugby_league_born_1991) "Michael Morgan (rugby league born 1991)") and Fijian-born [Semi Radradra](/wiki/Semi_Radradra "Semi Radradra") made their international debuts for Australia while [Dallin Watene-Zelezniak](/wiki/Dallin_Watene-Zelezniak "Dallin Watene-Zelezniak"), [Kenny Bromwich](/wiki/Kenny_Bromwich "Kenny Bromwich") and [Manu Maʻu](/wiki/Manu_Ma%CA%BBu "Manu Maʻu") made their international debuts for New Zealand.

## Women's Test

| Date | 6 May 2016 |
| Stadium | [Hunter Stadium](/wiki/Newcastle_International_Sports_Centre "Newcastle International Sports Centre") |
| Location | [Newcastle](/wiki/Newcastle,_New_South_Wales "Newcastle, New South Wales"), [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales"), [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia") |

| ← [2015](/wiki/2015_Anzac_Test#Women.27s_Test "2015 Anzac Test")  [2017](/wiki/2017_Anzac_Test#Women's_Test "2017 Anzac Test") → | |

A [Women's rugby league](/wiki/Women%27s_rugby_league "Women's rugby league") match between the [Australian Jillaroos](/wiki/Australian_Jillaroos "Australian Jillaroos") and New Zealand [Kiwi Ferns](/wiki/Kiwi_Ferns "Kiwi Ferns") will serve as the curtain-raiser for the main game.

New Zealand coach Alan Jackson named an 18-strong squad in preparation for the Trans-Tasman Test.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

A few days later, Australian coach Steve Folkes announced his 18-strong squad for the Trans-Tasman Test.[[7]](#cite_note-7)
